hawkish_phrases: a significant margin of excess demand is likely to build in the medium term
excess demand, to around 1¾% of potential GDP by the end of the forecast period
CPI inflation is projected to rise above the 2% target
CPI inflation reaches 2.4% by the end of the three-year forecast period
increases in interest rates, at a gradual pace and to a limited extent, would be appropriate
dovish_phrases: underlying growth appears to have slowed since 2018 to a rate below potential
a margin of excess supply persists over the first year of the projection
underlying output growth is subdued in the near term
substantial decline in advanced economies' forward interest rates and a material loosening in financial conditions
The monetary policy response to Brexit, whatever form it takes, will not be automatic and could be in either direction
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.75%, judging the existing stance appropriate while signaling that assuming a smooth Brexit and global recovery, increases in interest rates at a gradual pace would be appropriate as significant excess demand builds to 1¾% of potential GDP with inflation reaching 2.4%.
